mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate revenue contraction, causing investors to price in lower earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 20-day forward returns following a 0.2pp unemployment jump are statistically indistinguishable from baseline or exhibit a positive median return.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling consumer credit indicates unsustainable debt-fueled consumption that will reverse sharply, triggering a negative earnings revision cycle and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises while RSAFS falls in the same month
failure_condition: The signal fails if SPX forward returns are positive or neutral in more than 50% of instances where housing starts rise and consumer credit declines simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Deceleration in the industrial production index reflects weakening aggregate supply momentum and capacity utilization, which historically precedes profit margin compression and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if quarterly forward returns remain positive when the 3-month moving average of industrial production contracts for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands corporate revenue bases and improves earnings per share trajectories, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as momentum traders and institutional flows chase growth.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if weekly forward returns show no positive correlation with quarterly GDP growth acceleration, or if Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3.0 percent annually forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, elevating real interest rates and discounting future cash flows, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if equity forward returns are positive or statistically flat when inflation prints above 3.0 percent, indicating markets price in successful Fed inflation control.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in total nonfarm payrolls and stagnation in the unemployment rate reveals a labor market disconnect where job growth is offset by discouraged workers, signaling underlying economic fragility and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k while UNRATE remains unchanged
failure_condition: The signal fails if 60-day forward returns are positive when payrolls surge but unemployment fails to decline, proving the labor disconnect does not precede equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained acceleration in housing construction permits reflects strong consumer confidence and wealth effects that spill over into broader consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if weekly forward returns are negative or neutral when housing starts accelerate by more than 2 percent month-over-month.
